message:
feat(stdio): add fparseln (NetBSD libutil logical-line reader) (bd-q5wxt)

NetBSD libutil fparseln(stream, *len, *lineno, delim, flags) reads
a logical line from a FILE*, handling backslash line continuation,
comments, and escape processing per the libutil grammar. Returns a
malloc'd C string the caller frees, or NULL on EOF/error. Used by
NetBSD ftpd, mtree, pkgsrc, and many BSD config-file parsers.

* core::stdio::fparseln::fold_line takes one physical line plus the
  running output buffer and applies escape/comment/continuation
  rules per delim and FPARSELN_UNESC_* flags. Returns Done or
  Continue (when the line ends with an unescaped escape-then-
  separator pair). The four flag bits FPARSELN_UNESC_ESC/CONT/COMM
  /REST and the OR'd FPARSELN_UNESC_ALL match libutil's <util.h>
  values exactly.
* stdio_abi::fparseln drives a fgetc loop to gather each physical
  line, feeds them to fold_line until Done, then malloc's the
  result + writes *len + advances *lineno. NULL delim selects
  ['\\', '\n', '#'] defaults.

18 core unit tests covering: every escape combination, every flag
bit including UNESC_ALL raw read, comment-strip, multi-line
continuation chains, and edge cases (lone trailing escape without
separator must NOT trigger continuation; comment terminates before
continuation can fire). 8 abi integration tests on real /tmp
files: plain line, comment strip, two-line continuation with
lineno increment, EOF returns NULL, UNESC_ALL raw read, custom
delimiter triple, NULL stream guard, NULL len pointer safety.
Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
